title: leptospermum leucodendron
intro: Leptospermum leucodendron, also known as the Leptospermum or White Ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is a shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its small white flowers and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australia for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Leptospermum leucodendron can be prepared into various herbal forms, such as infusions, ointments, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues.
